followups.question: Which applications are in scope for sizing?
Who is the primary buyer you'll sell through first?
What obtainable horizon should Year-1 (YAM) assume?

ledger.label: Central Europe rack-PDU market, all segments/buyers

derivation.method: Top-down scope-down from the global rack-PDU base
derivation.expression: Global rack-PDU $2.81B (Grand View, 2025) × Europe ≈ 30% [est.; NA = 38% per same source] ≈ $843M ≈ €775M @0.92 (Jul 2026) × CE-7 = 39.8% of Europe colo MW (Statista 2025) ≈ €310M → curated €300M
derivation.crossCheck: Refinement cycle 1: the prior €495M ceiling (BIS/R&M €2.45B) measures PDUs+PSUs combined — a broader category growing 21% CAGR vs intelligent-PDU 10–12% (IndexBox). Contaminated ceiling removed; band top lowered to €360M.

provenance.rationale: No public Europe- or CE-scoped rack-PDU figure exists; the scope-down chain anchors the value, and refinement cycle 1 killed the inflated PDUs+PSUs cross-check ceiling.
provenance.promotionPath: Commission the IndexBox EU Rack PDUs report (country tables) to pin the EU base directly.

evidence.title: Data Center Rack PDU Market Size | Industry Report, 2033
evidence.excerpt: The global data center rack power distribution unit market size was estimated at USD 2.81 billion in 2025 and is projected to reach USD 5.87 billion by 2033... North America held 38.0% revenue share.

evidence.title: Europe Data Center PDUs and PSUs Market 2025–2035
evidence.excerpt: The Europe data center PDUs and PSUs market is projected to reach $20.05 billion by 2035 from $2.45 billion in 2024 — a broader category (PDUs + PSUs) used as the cross-check ceiling.

evidence.title: IndexBox — Rack PDUs Market in the European Union (country tables)
evidence.excerpt: The directly-scoped EU rack-PDU report exists but its headline figure is paywalled — commissioning it would collapse the ±40% band.

derivation.expression: DE colo 1,737 MW ÷ CE-7 colo 3,451 MW (Statista 2025) = 0.503 → 0.50 (normalized)
derivation.crossCheck: Installed-IT-load method (BMWK >2,700 MW ÷ CE-7 installed) → 0.42 — sets the low end

provenance.rationale: Two independent capacity methods bracket 0.42–0.53; colo-scope figure is primary → inferred.
provenance.promotionPath: Reconcile colo vs total-installed scopes with one 2025 vendor-revenue split → verified.

evidence.title: Europe colocation data center IT power supply by country, 2024–2031
evidence.excerpt: Germany 1,737 MW of colocation and scale-colocation IT power in 2025 — the largest CE base (NL 951, CH 274, PL 228, CZ 79, AT 68).

evidence.title: Status and development of the German data centre landscape
evidence.excerpt: With over 2,000 data centres and an installed IT power demand of over 2,700 MW, Germany is already the largest centre for digital infrastructure in Europe.

derivation.expression: NL colo 951 MW ÷ CE-7 colo 3,451 MW (Statista 2025) = 0.276 → 0.28 (normalized)
derivation.crossCheck: Dutch Data Center Association: national colo 924 MW (2024) — consistent scale

provenance.rationale: Statista and the national association agree within ~3% → inferred, triangulated.

evidence.title: Europe colocation data center IT power supply by country, 2024–2031
evidence.excerpt: Netherlands 951 MW of colocation and scale-colocation IT power in 2025.

evidence.title: Ten years of State of the Dutch Data Centers
evidence.excerpt: In 2024, the total colocation capacity reached 924 megawatts... the Amsterdam Metropolitan Area still accounts for approximately 70% of colocation capacity.

derivation.expression: PL colo 228 MW ÷ CE-7 colo 3,451 MW (Statista 2025) = 0.066 → 0.07 (normalized)
derivation.crossCheck: Installed-IT-load method (Mordor 660 MW, 2025) → ~0.10 — scopes diverge, band widened

provenance.rationale: Colo vs installed-load scopes diverge ~1.5x for the fast-growing Polish base → inferred, wide band.
provenance.promotionPath: Adopt one scope (PMR commercial-DC series) across all CE countries → narrow band.

evidence.title: Europe colocation data center IT power supply by country, 2024–2031
evidence.excerpt: Poland 228 MW of colocation and scale-colocation IT power in 2025.

evidence.title: Poland Data Center Market — size & installed IT load
evidence.excerpt: Base Year Market Size (2025): 660 megawatt installed IT load, growing to 998.34 MW by 2030 (7.28% CAGR).

derivation.expression: CH colo 274 MW ÷ CE-7 colo 3,451 MW (Statista 2025) = 0.079 → 0.08 (normalized)
derivation.crossCheck: Installed-base method (Mordor 850.6 MW incl. self-operated) → ~0.13 — Swiss market is self-operation-heavy

provenance.rationale: Large self-operated share (CBRE Suisse: 340 MW commercial only) makes scope choice decisive → inferred, wide band.

evidence.title: Europe colocation data center IT power supply by country, 2024–2031
evidence.excerpt: Switzerland 274 MW of colocation and scale-colocation IT power in 2025.

evidence.title: CBRE Suisse — Swiss data-center market capacity
evidence.excerpt: Commercial capacity (excluding self-operated infrastructure) is 340 MW across 121 sites; ~61% (209 MW) concentrated in the Zurich region.

derivation.expression: CZ colo 79 MW ÷ CE-7 colo 3,451 MW (Statista 2025) = 0.023 → 0.02 (normalized)

provenance.rationale: Both capacity methods land at ~0.02 → inferred, triangulated.

evidence.title: Czechia Data Center Market — volume
evidence.excerpt: The Czechia data center market size stood at 152.67 MW in 2025 and is forecast to reach 183.47 MW by 2030.

derivation.expression: AT colo 68 MW ÷ CE-7 colo 3,451 MW (Statista 2025) = 0.020 → 0.02 (normalized)

provenance.rationale: Colo vs installed scopes diverge (Vienna hosts >80% of national capacity) → inferred.

evidence.title: Austria Data Center Market — volume
evidence.excerpt: Base Year Market Size (2025): 207 megawatt installed base, reaching 342.54 MW by 2031 (8.94% CAGR).

derivation.expression: HU 18 + RO 27 + SK 14 + SI 6 + HR 17 + BG 32 = 114 MW ÷ CE-7 colo 3,451 MW (Statista 2025) = 0.033 → 0.03 (residual, normalized)

provenance.rationale: Sum of small itemised markets from one source; fast CEE growth (Romania +40% of upcoming CEE capacity) adds uncertainty.
provenance.promotionPath: Break out RO/HU explicitly once either exceeds ~5% of the CE base.

evidence.title: Europe colocation data center IT power supply by country, 2024–2031
evidence.excerpt: Hungary 18, Romania 27, Slovakia 14, Slovenia 6, Croatia 17, Bulgaria 32 MW of colocation IT power in 2025.

derivation.crossCheck: Synergy Q1-2025 vintage: 44% — brackets the band

provenance.rationale: Multiple Synergy vintages (41→48% through 2025) agree on trajectory; global split applied to CE → inferred.
provenance.promotionPath: Replace with a Europe-scoped capacity split when CBRE/Synergy publish one.

evidence.title: Hyperscale operators to account for 67% of all data center capacity by 2031
evidence.excerpt: They now account for 48% of the worldwide capacity of all data centers... non-hyperscale colocation capacity accounting for another 20%... leaves enterprise on-premise data centers with just 32%.

evidence.title: Hyperscale operators and colocation continue to drive huge changes
evidence.excerpt: They now account for 44% of the worldwide capacity of all data centers (Q1 2025 vintage).

provenance.rationale: Single publisher's capacity series (20–22% across vintages), global scope applied to CE → inferred.

evidence.title: Hyperscale operators to account for 67% of all data center capacity by 2031
evidence.excerpt: Non-hyperscale colocation capacity accounting for another 20% of total capacity (end-2025).

derivation.crossCheck: Refinement cycle 1: NVIDIA's B300 reference architectures ship rack PDUs in both AC and DC variants (3 per rack / 160 per pod) — GPU growth is PDU-consuming. Value confirmed; downside band covers dense busbar deployments.

provenance.rationale: Single publisher's capacity series, but the 'GPU pods skip PDUs' doubt was refuted by vendor reference architectures (refinement cycle 1) → value confirmed with a downside band.

evidence.title: Hyperscale operators to account for 67% of all data center capacity by 2031
evidence.excerpt: That leaves enterprise on-premise data centers with just 32% of the total — down from 56% in 2018, heading to 19% by 2031.

derivation.expression: No public edge-share-of-total figure; edge market ≈ $12–19B absolute (GVR/Mordor 2024-25) vs total DC market → ~5% carve-out

provenance.rationale: Edge is sized in absolute $ only (never as % of total DC) across all cached sources → widest relative band.
provenance.promotionPath: Derive edge share from a single-source pair (edge $ ÷ total DC $) at matching vintages.

evidence.title: Edge Data Center Market Size | Industry Report
evidence.excerpt: Edge data center market sized at $12.36B (2024) in absolute terms — no share-of-total-DC figure published.

derivation.expression: No clean telecom share exists (FMI's 34.6% conflates IT & telecom on the power sub-market); telecom central-office PDU demand estimated at ~4% carve-out

provenance.rationale: Smallest segment; the only cached figure conflates IT with telecom → estimate pending a clean split.
provenance.promotionPath: Source a telecom-only share from an operator-capex breakdown.

evidence.title: Telecom power systems market context
evidence.excerpt: Telecom power infrastructure sized separately from data-center power; no telecom-share-of-DC-market figure published.

derivation.expression: Hyperscale+colo ≈ 62% of capacity (Synergy) and large operators buy direct → dominant buyer ≈ 0.40 of PDU spend

provenance.rationale: Report segmentations name the channels (direct/OEM/distributor) but publish no percentages → internal estimate.
provenance.promotionPath: Validate buyer mix with 3 CE operator/distributor interviews.

evidence.title: Data center growth driving power equipment sales
evidence.excerpt: One leading supplier reported data centers as its biggest growth driver, accounting for 30% of orders in 2025 — qualitative channel context only.

evidence.title: CE operator & distributor interviews
evidence.excerpt: Three structured interviews would replace this qualitative split with a sourced one.

provenance.rationale: Mirrors the enterprise capacity share (Synergy 32% × partial direct purchasing); no public channel split → internal estimate.
provenance.promotionPath: Validate buyer mix with 3 CE operator/distributor interviews.

provenance.rationale: OEM/ODM white-label supply is well documented qualitatively (rack-integrator channel) but unquantified → internal estimate.
provenance.promotionPath: Validate buyer mix with 3 CE operator/distributor interviews.

provenance.rationale: Master-distributor models exist (documented qualitatively) but indirect share is unquantified → internal estimate.
provenance.promotionPath: Validate buyer mix with 3 CE operator/distributor interviews.

derivation.expression: No public SAM/TAM benchmark for B2B hardware exists; EU cert stack (EN 50600, ecodesign 2019/424, CE/TÜV) justifies < 1.0 but doesn't quantify 0.55

provenance.rationale: Assumption leaf — channel + regulatory reach; EU certification barriers documented qualitatively only.
provenance.promotionPath: Bottom-up channel-coverage model (named accounts × reachable share) → sourced serviceable %.

evidence.title: EU ecodesign requirements for servers and data storage (2019/424)
evidence.excerpt: EU ecodesign and certification requirements gate which demand is regulatory-clear for a new entrant — the qualitative basis of the serviceable haircut.

derivation.expression: Prospeo assigns 1% of SAM to YEAR 1 (3% and 5% are Years 2–3) — the prior 3% read the Year-2 figure as Year 1. Corrected to 1%; the Tractian comparable (first-year ~$1.2M against a multi-$B market) sits below 1%.
derivation.crossCheck: Early-procurement mechanism (DC electrical gear locked 12–24mo before fit-out) independently supports ≤2%; open 2026 EU rack-PDU tenders keep 2–3% inside the band.

provenance.rationale: Refinement cycle 1 caught the benchmark-period misread — the value now follows the source's own Year-1 figure, band 0.5–3%.
provenance.promotionPath: Replace with pipeline-based model once first CE deals close (win-rate × cycle time × capacity).

evidence.title: Addressable markets — SOM benchmarks
evidence.excerpt: SOM benchmarks that won't get you laughed out of the room: 1% of SAM in Year 1, 3% in Year 2, 5% in Year 3.

evidence.title: Serviceable Obtainable Market (SOM) — early-stage ranges
evidence.excerpt: For early-stage companies, SOM is typically 1-5% of SAM. For growing companies with product-market fit, SOM can reach 10-20% of SAM.

derivation.expression: Global rack-PDU: 9.7% (GVR 2026-33), 8.96% (Mordor 2026-31); EU rack panels 5–8%, Eastern Europe 9–12% (IndexBox) → consensus 9%

provenance.rationale: Three independent publishers bracket 8–10% for rack-PDU; CE mixes slower EU + faster CEE growth → inferred.

evidence.title: Data Center Rack PDU Market — growth forecast
evidence.excerpt: Growing at a CAGR of 9.7% from 2026 to 2033.

evidence.title: Data Center Rack PDU Market — size & share analysis
evidence.excerpt: Reach USD 4.62 billion by 2031, growing at a CAGR of 8.96% over 2026-2031.

evidence.title: Eastern Europe Rack Power Distribution Panels — growth
evidence.excerpt: Expanding at a compound annual rate of 9–12% between 2026 and 2035, driven by rapid data centre construction in Poland, Romania, and the Baltic states.

derivation.expression: Only hard datapoint: #1 vendor (APC/Schneider) at 15.8%; top-3 identities consistent (Schneider, Vertiv, Eaton); 'medium/semi-consolidated' concentration → CR3 ≈ 0.55 (est. 0.45–0.65)

provenance.rationale: No published CR3 for rack-PDU; concentration described only qualitatively across all cached sources → estimate.
provenance.promotionPath: Obtain the Omdia rack-PDU vendor-share table (2024 base) for a sourced CR3.

evidence.title: Data Center Rack PDU Market — competitive landscape
evidence.excerpt: APC by Schneider Electric led competitive landscape with 15.8% market share.

evidence.title: Data Center Rack PDU Market — key players
evidence.excerpt: Key players: Schneider Electric SE, Vertiv Group Corp., Eaton Corporation plc, ABB Ltd, Legrand SA. Market concentration: Medium.

evidence.title: Omdia rack-PDU vendor share table
evidence.excerpt: A vendor-share table (2024 base) would replace this extrapolation with a sourced CR3.

boundaryExclusions.reason: Substitute power-distribution architecture with a different spec and buyer. Including it would expand the TAM but requires a substitution-risk research pass.
Rack-internal DC distribution — adjacent, but outside the rack-PDU boundary as currently scoped.
DC-distribution substitute; excluded pending a boundary-lever research pass.
Other regions are a separate market-sizing run, not part of this scope.
